name: Boyan Slat
quote: The oceans are not too polluted to clean. But we need to act with urgency and at the scale the problem demands.

name: Sylvia Earle
quote: No ocean, no life. No blue, no green. The ocean is the cornerstone of Earth's life support system.

name: Ove Hoegh-Guldberg
role: Lead Coral Reef Scientist, University of Queensland / IPCC Lead Author
quote: We need to decide whether we want coral reefs to be part of our future. Right now, our emissions trajectory says we don't.

name: Sir David Attenborough
quote: Surely we have a responsibility to care for our blue planet. The future of humanity — and indeed all life on Earth — now depends on us.

name: Richard Spinrad
quote: NOAA's Coral Reef Watch data confirms this is the most extensive and severe bleaching event we have ever recorded. The time for incremental action has passed.

name: Tommy Remengesau Jr.
role: Former President of Palau; Global Ocean Champion
quote: The ocean is our life support system. For island peoples, protecting it is not optional — it is existential. The world must treat it as such.

name: Monica Medina
role: Former US Assistant Secretary of State, Oceans & International Environmental Affairs
quote: The High Seas Treaty is the most significant achievement in ocean governance since the adoption of UNCLOS. It fills a legal void that has existed for 40 years.

name: Inger Andersen
role: Executive Director, United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)
quote: Plastic pollution is a full-cycle crisis. We must address it from production to disposal — no voluntary pledge or technology fix alone can substitute for a legally binding global treaty.

name: Rena Lee
role: Singapore Ambassador; President, BBNJ Intergovernmental Conference (UN)
quote: For the first time, we have a legal framework to protect the 64% of the ocean that lies beyond national jurisdiction. It took 20 years of negotiations, but we got there.

name: Marco Lambertini
quote: Protecting 30% of our oceans by 2030 is both achievable and essential to reversing biodiversity collapse. Every MPA designation matters — but only if it's enforced.

name: Andrew Sharpless
quote: The ocean doesn't need our charity — it needs us to stop treating it as a trash dump and a fishery without limits. Science-based management of fisheries alone could feed a billion people.

name: Guy Platten
role: Secretary General, International Chamber of Shipping
quote: Shipping has zero tolerance for ocean dumping. We are committed to net-zero emissions by 2050, but we need consistent, internationally harmonised regulation — not a patchwork of national rules.
